fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer audiologist license to tennessee, audiologist compact tennessee, tennessee audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Tennessee Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series offered by the Educational Testing Service

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Tennessee.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Visit the Tennessee e-Licensure Portal (licensure.tn.gov), create a new user account, and complete the online application for Audiologist licensure. Pay the required non-refundable application fee of $75.

steps.title: Submit Academic and Exam Verifications
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying a doctoral degree in audiology from an ASHA-accredited program, to be sent directly from your university to the Board. Also, ensure official scores from the Praxis Exam in Audiology (5030) are sent directly from ETS to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the e-Licensure Portal to register for and complete a criminal background check through IdentoGO, a state-approved vendor.

steps.title: Pass the Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Access and successfully complete the Tennessee Jurisprudence Exam for Audiologists, which is available online through the e-Licensure Portal, covering state laws and regulations.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure Issuance
steps.description: The Tennessee Board of Communication Disorders and Sciences will review all submitted documents, exam results, and background check. Once all requirements are met and verified, your Audiologist license will be issued through the e-Licensure Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tennessee participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Tennessee without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Tennessee
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Tennessee, applicants must register and complete the application through the Tennessee e-Licensure Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Access the Tennessee e-Licensure Portal, click 'Register' to create a new account using your email and password, then follow the prompts to complete your personal profile before starting an application.
